title: Presentation Components
text: Presentation Components Presentation components are used to present and gather information. They are primarily used to provide the user interface of web and desktop applications, but can also be used for reports. Uniface provides the following presentation components: Dynamic Server Pages for web and mobile applications. Static Server Pages for web applications. Forms for Windows and character mode applications. Reports for formatting and printing reports. Presentation components enable you to: Define widgets for data input and display. Each field can be associated with a widget, such as an edit box for data entry, a check box for a Boolean value, a drop-down list for selecting a value, and so on. Arrange the fields and other elements in a layout. Depending on the component, it is possible for one component to contain other components, to present fields in a table or grid, to set the dimensions of components and fields, or use elements such as lines and frames to group fields. Style the component and the objects it contains using widget properties. For example, you can set colors. borders, and fonts, control the title bar of form windows. Many properties can be set dynamically to respond to events like getting focus. The type of component imposes restrictions on what you can do in terms of both the presentation and the functionality. Thus dynamic server pages enable more responsive web pages than static server pages. Server pages can only use HTML-based widgets and need to be coded for a stateless environment. Reports cannot have widgets intended for input from the user and support specialized frames for printing headers and footers and managing page breaks. Forms and reports can access the database directly and incorporate business logic. However, to create scalable applications you should limit the functionality of presentation components to gathering and presenting data, and delegate business logic and data access to services. In server pages, the split between the presentation and logic layers is much more strictly defined. Related Links Widgets In Uniface, widgets are user interface controls. They can display data values, control how values are modified, and determine the appearance of those objects. Most widgets are field-level widgets. Uniface Component Types Uniface components handle most of the processing of a Uniface application. The different types of Uniface component are intended for different platforms and serve different functions. Presentation Tier In a three-tier application, the presentation tier is the logical group of components that provide a user interface. In a Uniface web application, these components are dynamic and static server pages, in a desktop application, they are forms and reports. Properties for Styling the User Interface Each object that can be viewed in the user interface has a collection of properties that can be used to control what it looks like, and some other aspects of its visual behavior.
